sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Good progress! After clicking the cross at (19, 30):
- G border appears around it (boundary [[17, 29], ...])
- g marks appear at [[18, 42], [18, 43]], [[19, 41], [20, 41]], [[19, 44], [20, 44]], [[21, 42], [21, 43]]
These g marks form a border around the cell at rows 19-20, cols 42-43. This is the position of the cross at center=(25, 42)! Wait no, that cross is at row 25, col 42. The g marks are at row 19-20, col 42-43.
Looking at the g mark boundaries:
- [[18, 42], [18, 43]] - top edge
- [[19, 41], [20, 41]] - left edge
- [[19, 44], [20, 44]] - right edge
- [[21, 42], [21, 43]] - bottom edge
This forms a border around the cell at rows 19-20, cols 42-43. Looking at the image, this is in the top grid, row 2 (bottom row), col 5-6 area.
Wait, let me check the cross positions:
- Cross at center=(25, 42): boundary [[24, 42], [24, 43], [25, 44], [26, 44], [27, 43], [27, 42], [26, 41], [25, 41]] - this is at rows 25-26, cols 42-43
The g marks are at rows 18-21, cols 41-44, surrounding the cell at rows 19-20, cols 42-43.
Looking at the image, this is the empty cell in the top grid's bottom row, right side (where cross 4 was originally).
So the pattern is:
1. Click a cross → G border on it, g marks on an adjacent empty cell
2. Click the g-marked cell → cross moves there
Let me click the g-marked cell at row 19, col 42 to move the cross there.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Clicking a cross shows G border on it and g marks on an adjacent empty cell (the destination). Clicking the g-marked cell moves the cross there. This is a "move cross to adjacent cell" mechanic.
Plan: Click the g-marked cell at row 19, col 42 to move the cross there.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Click the g-marked cell at row 19, col 42
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 19, 'col': 42}])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
colors = {}
for n in seg['nodes']:
    c = n['color']
    colors[c] = colors.get(c, 0) + 1
print("Colors:", colors)

# Check green crosses
N_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N']
print(f"\nGreen crosses ({len(N_nodes)}):")
for n in N_nodes:
    rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
    cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
    print(f"  center=({(min(rows)+max(rows))//2}, {(min(cols)+max(cols))//2})")

# Check if level completed
print("Level completed:", last_action_result.get('level_completed', False))

# Look at the area
after_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nTop grid area:")
for i in range(17, 23):
    print(f"  Row {i}: {after_lines[i][10:52]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
